recommended_action: Download ECU fault history. Perform VSV full-range functional test. If actuator binding found, replace and re-rig per AMM before dispatch.
notes: Check for ice ingestion if fault occurred during descent through icing conditions.

recommended_action: Identify leak source — check fuel manifold, supply lines, and fuel nozzle fittings. Isolate affected circuit. Replace leaking component and pressure-test before return to service.
notes: Check fuel nozzle O-rings — they harden and crack with age.

symptoms: oil fumes in cabin bleed air
recommended_action: Sample oil and send for spectrometric analysis. Check chip detector for metallic debris. Inspect oil cooler, seals, and scavenge lines. Top up oil within approved limits.
notes: Monitor oil quantity trending. Loss >0.3 qt/hr requires investigation.

recommended_action: Ground aircraft immediately. Perform full fuel system leak check per AMM. Do not dispatch with any active fuel leak.
notes: Fuel imbalance >500 lbs unexplained by normal use requires investigation.

symptoms: compressor stall at specific N1 range
recommended_action: Download ECU fault history. Perform VSV full-range functional test. If actuator binding found, replace and re-rig per AMM before dispatch.
notes: LVDT signal dropout is a common root cause — check connector integrity.

recommended_action: Immediate borescope inspection required. Ground aircraft if blade tip loss is confirmed. HPT blade replacement mandatory before next flight.
notes: Common after high-cycle operation in sandy environments.

recommended_action: Identify leak source — check fuel manifold, supply lines, and fuel nozzle fittings. Isolate affected circuit. Replace leaking component and pressure-test before return to service.
notes: Inspect fuel manifold joints during every 'C' check as a preventive measure.

recommended_action: Reduce thrust immediately. Inspect compressor blades for FOD or damage. Check bleed air valves and variable stator vane schedule. Do not dispatch until root cause found.
notes: ECU fault code P0420 or similar may be logged. Pull ACARS data for confirmation.

recommended_action: Immediate borescope inspection required. Ground aircraft if blade tip loss is confirmed. HPT blade replacement mandatory before next flight.
notes: Monitor EGT trend data over the last 50 cycles for confirmation.

recommended_action: Ground aircraft immediately. Perform full fuel system leak check per AMM. Do not dispatch with any active fuel leak.
notes: Inspect fuel manifold joints during every 'C' check as a preventive measure.

symptoms: compressor stall at specific N1 range
recommended_action: Download ECU fault history. Perform VSV full-range functional test. If actuator binding found, replace and re-rig per AMM before dispatch.
notes: Check for ice ingestion if fault occurred during descent through icing conditions.

symptoms: visible fan blade nick or damage
recommended_action: Perform fan trim balance. Inspect all fan blades for nicks, dents, or FOD damage. Check spinner for cracks. Re-balance fan assembly if within limits.
notes: FOD strikes are the most common cause — inspect inlet carefully.

recommended_action: Perform full hot section borescope. Measure EGT margin against baseline. If margin is <20°C, schedule engine removal for shop visit.
notes: EGT margin is the primary indicator of hot section health.

recommended_action: Perform fan trim balance. Inspect all fan blades for nicks, dents, or FOD damage. Check spinner for cracks. Re-balance fan assembly if within limits.
notes: FOD strikes are the most common cause — inspect inlet carefully.

recommended_action: Perform full hot section borescope. Measure EGT margin against baseline. If margin is <20°C, schedule engine removal for shop visit.
notes: Review last borescope images for combustor liner condition.

recommended_action: Reduce thrust immediately. Inspect compressor blades for FOD or damage. Check bleed air valves and variable stator vane schedule. Do not dispatch until root cause found.
notes: ECU fault code P0420 or similar may be logged. Pull ACARS data for confirmation.

recommended_action: Download ECU fault history. Perform VSV full-range functional test. If actuator binding found, replace and re-rig per AMM before dispatch.
notes: LVDT signal dropout is a common root cause — check connector integrity.

recommended_action: Ground aircraft immediately. Chip detector positive means potential bearing failure. Perform oil spectral analysis. Do not operate engine until bearing inspection is complete.
notes: Metallic particle shape indicates failure mode — flakes suggest spalling, fines suggest wear.

symptoms: visible fan blade nick or damage
recommended_action: If vibration >8 mils, ground aircraft. Inspect fan blades per AMM limits. Replace damaged blade and perform full fan balance before dispatch.
notes: Vibration asymmetry between left and right accelerometers confirms fan source.

recommended_action: Pull engine for shop visit. Inspect No.1, No.3, and No.4 bearings. Replace all bearings in the affected sump. Flush oil system and replace filter.
notes: Chip detector activations should ALWAYS be treated as serious until proven otherwise.

recommended_action: Immediate borescope inspection required. Ground aircraft if blade tip loss is confirmed. HPT blade replacement mandatory before next flight.
notes: Common after high-cycle operation in sandy environments.

recommended_action: Ground aircraft immediately. Chip detector positive means potential bearing failure. Perform oil spectral analysis. Do not operate engine until bearing inspection is complete.
notes: Vibration signature analysis (frequency spectrum) can pinpoint which bearing is failing.

recommended_action: Sample oil and send for spectrometric analysis. Check chip detector for metallic debris. Inspect oil cooler, seals, and scavenge lines. Top up oil within approved limits.
notes: Monitor oil quantity trending. Loss >0.3 qt/hr requires investigation.

recommended_action: Trend monitor EGT over next 10 cycles. If degradation continues, pull engine for HPT and combustor inspection. Check for liner burnthrough or NGV cracking.
notes: Review last borescope images for combustor liner condition.

recommended_action: Reduce thrust immediately. Inspect compressor blades for FOD or damage. Check bleed air valves and variable stator vane schedule. Do not dispatch until root cause found.
notes: Often triggered by FOD ingestion or icing conditions.

recommended_action: Perform borescope inspection of HPT blades. Replace blades exceeding wear limits. Check turbine clearances. Log EGT margin trend before return to service.
notes: EGT margin reduction of >15°C from baseline is a red flag.

recommended_action: Trend monitor EGT over next 10 cycles. If degradation continues, pull engine for HPT and combustor inspection. Check for liner burnthrough or NGV cracking.
notes: EGT margin is the primary indicator of hot section health.

notes: VSV faults often trigger ACARS EICAS message — pull full fault history.
